title: Pneumonanthe frigida
intro: Pneumonanthe frigida, also known as Yellow Marsh Marigold, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as skin irritations and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pneumonanthe frigida include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which have been identified as responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Pneumonanthe frigida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Pneumonanthe, which is a small group of plants endemic to the Himalayas. The species Pneumonanthe frigida is a rare and endangered alpine plant that grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. This plant is also known as the Himalayan gentian or frigid gentian. Pneumonanthe frigida is considered a threatened species due to its limited range and habitat degradation.
